window: last ~24 months (curated via the azure-machine-learning-complaints skill)
note: Curated by the azure-machine-learning-complaints skill from PUBLIC sources: genuine bug reports filed by real users in Microsoft's official Azure/azureml-examples GitHub repo (each labelled 'bug' or 'enhancement' by the repo), plus a public Hacker News thread. Every item links to its original public post and is presented as user-reported. Because 'ML' is generic, only items that clearly reference Azure Machine Learning (managed online endpoints, AML compute, Azure ML Studio, the azure-ai-ml SDK / az ml CLI) are included. Most are single-source so verified=false, but unlike keyword auto-collection these were hand-reviewed. Public HN mentions of Azure ML were overwhelmingly old marketing/launch posts (2014-2019); the genuine problem reports below come almost entirely from the product's GitHub issue tracker.

complaints.complaint: A user reported that an Azure Machine Learning managed online endpoint configured to auto-scale on request volume takes far longer to scale up than expected — roughly 12-18 minutes, versus the 1-2 minutes they see from comparable services like VM scale sets — and asked how to speed it up.
complaints.quote: With endpoint, scaling takes a long time, about 12-18 minutes.

complaints.complaint: A user reported repeated HTTP 500 errors when calling Azure Machine Learning serverless model endpoints (Meta-Llama-3.1-405B and Cohere Command R+) while following the official langchain example, with many 500s coming back from the endpoints.
complaints.quote: There are many logs reporting 500s coming from the 2 following URLs ... Actual behavior: 500 errors

complaints.complaint: A user reported that an identical distributed PyTorch training job on Azure Machine Learning compute succeeds on an A100 cluster but fails completely on an H100 (Standard_NC80adis_H100_v5) cluster with NCCL networking errors ('Connection closed by remote peer').
complaints.quote: The job on A100 cluster succeeds without obvious error. The job on H100 cluster failed completely due to some NCCL errors.

complaints.complaint: A user reported that an Azure Machine Learning compute cluster node of size Standard_NC40ads_H100_v5 is provisioned with only 128 GB of disk, far less than the ~3576 GiB the VM size advertises, making it impossible to fit and train medium-or-larger models on the node.
complaints.quote: The node has only 128 GB of storage so it's not possible to use it to train or run even medium models (like Qwen3-Coder-Next).

complaints.complaint: A user reported that an Azure Machine Learning fine-tuning (DreamBooth text-to-image) job fails with 'AzureMLException: Failed to flush task queue within 300.0 seconds' (a ResourceExhausted/Timeout error), and noted it was unclear how to raise the timeout on a compute instance.
complaints.quote: Encountered an internal ACFT error. Error Message/Code: AzureMLException: Failed to flush task queue within 300.0 seconds.

complaints.complaint: A user reported that the Azure ML CLI (az ml extension) rejects the documented 'msi_resource_id' field for managed-identity pipeline jobs with 'TypeError: ManagedIdentityConfiguration.__init__() got an unexpected keyword argument msi_resource_id', i.e. the schema and the SDK/CLI code disagree.
complaints.quote: TypeError: ManagedIdentityConfiguration.__init__() got an unexpected keyword argument 'msi_resource_id'. Did you mean 'resource_id'?

complaints.complaint: A user reported that once the Azure Container Registry admin login is disabled (a security best practice), Azure Machine Learning serverless compute can no longer pull the environment image because a managed identity cannot be attached to serverless compute, blocking their pipeline.
complaints.quote: As soon as I disable the admin login I cannot use the serverless compute as I have to attach the managed identity which is impossible for serverless compute.

complaints.complaint: A user reported that when an Azure Machine Learning pipeline is changed but keeps the same name, the pipeline component in the registry is not updated when redeploying a batch endpoint (the changes silently don't take effect), and that there is no way to delete a pipeline component from ML Studio.
complaints.quote: The pipeline component is not updated ... Also, there is no way to delete the pipeline component from the ML studio.

complaints.complaint: A user reported that the official Azure Machine Learning feature-store tutorial notebook fails at the data-loading step because the sample blob storage account ('azuremlexampledata.blob.core.windows.net') now requires authentication instead of being anonymously accessible, breaking the documented walkthrough.
complaints.quote: No credentials found for account azuremlexampledata.blob.core.windows.net ... its container data is not accessible using anonymous credentials.

complaints.complaint: A user reported that creating an Azure Machine Learning environment from a base image hosted in a private Azure Container Registry and publishing it to an ML registry fails with 'Authentication failed for container registry', even though docker pull and acr login work and publishing the same image to a workspace (rather than a registry) succeeds.
complaints.quote: Error is returned : Authentication failed for container registry myacr. Code: UserError

complaints.complaint: On Hacker News, a developer weighing Azure Machine Learning's serverless compute for ML training said the managed options are hard to evaluate and worried about cost: with self-managed instances they get cheap spot pricing but often forget to shut instances down, which 'ofc is quite expensive'.
complaints.quote: I often forget to shut down the instances which ofc is quite expensive. I'm having a hard time understanding which of the offerings are worth using.
